park_name: BIG4 Apollo Bay Pisces Holiday Park
rationale_top3: Outstanding entertainment facilities scoring maximum points (20/20), indicating excellent amenities and activities for families Exceptional natural setting (18/20) on the Great Ocean Road, providing beautiful surroundings and outdoor experiences Exemplary cleanliness standards (15/15) demonstrating well-maintained facilities throughout the park
rationale_honourable: Strong value proposition (12/15) offering good quality for money Prime Great Ocean Road location (8/8) with excellent accessibility to regional attractions Highly positive guest sentiment (10/10) reflecting consistent visitor satisfaction
water_fun: Expected to have quality water-based entertainment given the maximum entertainment score and coastal location
kids_play: Excellent children's facilities and entertainment options evidenced by the perfect entertainment score
pet_detail: Pets are not permitted at this park
best_for: Families with children, Great Ocean Road tourists, nature lovers, and guests prioritizing cleanliness and entertainment facilities

rationale_top3: BIG4 Anglesea Holiday Park consistently earns praise as one of Australia's premier family holiday destinations, with multiple families declaring it "the best holiday park we've ever stayed at" and "one of the best run family parks" after visiting 50+ parks nationwide. The indoor heated pool complex with waterslide stands as the jewel in the crown—described as "surprisingly large," "unreal and really warm," and perfect year-round regardless of weather. Families rave about the jumping pillow, multiple playgrounds including toddler areas, games rooms, and special touches like animal statues, resident kangaroos and cockatoos creating magical wildlife encounters right at cabin doors. The park's immaculate cleanliness sets it apart, with guests repeatedly calling it "the cleanest Big 4 I've ever stayed in" and praising individual bathroom facilities that feel like private ensuites rather than typical campground blocks. Staff receive exceptional recognition for going above and beyond—fixing broken setups, organizing emergency cabin upgrades, even spending two hours helping repair a guest's car. The modern, well-equipped cabins and unique safari glamping tents provide comfortable bases, while the compact layout means "nothing is too out of the way" for families with small children, creating a safe environment where kids roam freely while parents relax.
rationale_honourable: Great Ocean Road location provides excellent access to coastal touring while Anglesea town shops and cafes are within easy 5-10 minute walk. Special holiday events including science shows, magic shows, and reptile encounters add extra entertainment value beyond the permanent facilities.
water_fun: Large indoor heated pool, waterslide, baby pool
kids_play: Multiple playgrounds, jumping pillow, games room, toddler area
best_for: This park excels for families with children aged 0-10 who prioritize on-site entertainment, year-round swimming facilities, and a compact safe layout over beach proximity or spacious caravan sites.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Anglesea Holiday Park
SCORE: 83/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-19
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 121

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Exceptional on-site facilities dominate reviews with the "surprisingly large" indoor heated pool and waterslide described as "unreal" and a clear standout. Families praise the jumping pillow, multiple playgrounds for different ages, toddler room with toys, games room with ping pong and arcade, giant chess, and special events including science shows, magic shows, reptile encounters, and food trucks during school holidays creating a "kids roam freely and safely" environment.

Nature (15/20) — While the park offers delightful wildlife encounters with resident kangaroos appearing at cabin doors and cockatoos throughout the grounds, creating "spectacular" moments for children, the natural environment scores moderately. The beach requires a 10-15 minute walk which multiple reviews note is "not really within walking distance with kids" and the park sits in town rather than bushland, though it provides good access to Great Ocean Road touring and a riverside bike trail.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Consistently rated "the cleanest Big 4 I've ever stayed in" with "immaculately maintained" facilities throughout. The individual bathroom rooms are praised as "best I've seen" and "spotlessly clean," cabins described as "immaculate" and "perfectly clean," and grounds "well manicured and clean all the time." Minor points deducted for occasional reports of bathrooms getting dirty quickly during peak periods and one report of filthy carpet and limited supplies in a unit.

Value (11/15) — Reviews show mixed value perception with several praising it as "cheaper than the next two sites" and appreciating included amenities, but criticism emerges for peak pricing ($460/night for 3-bedroom units) and additional costs (extra towels $5, ping pong balls $1, arcade games $2). One extended-stay family felt it "wasn't worth" the forced longer booking and costs, though most acknowledge "you get what you pay for" with the quality facilities.

Site Size (6/10) — Significant recurring complaint about "very cramped campsite" conditions where caravans "couldn't even put up our awning due to no space" and guests felt "like rabbits in cages." Multiple reviews warn sites are a "tight fit" requiring good reversing skills, though the compact layout is praised by cabin guests as family-friendly with everything close. Powered sites on astroturf help with mud management but reinforce the confined feeling.

Sentiment (10/10) — Overwhelmingly positive with extraordinary loyalty: "we immediately booked again next year," "our go-to park for 10+ years," "we will be staying here for years to come," and multiple "can't wait to come back" declarations. Families frequently state it's their favourite Big4 and best park experience, with children "haven't stopped asking to go back." Even most critical reviews acknowledge good aspects, and the passionate positive reviews far outweigh negatives.

Location (9/10) — Great Ocean Road location praised as "perfect for exploring" and "unbeatable" with Anglesea town shops, cafes and IGA within 5-10 minute walk. Close to Melbourne (1.5 hours) makes it ideal for weekend getaways. However, beach access requires 10-15 minute walk which isn't ideal with young children or beach gear, and one review notes driving 20 minutes to Torquay for major supermarkets. Overall considered "centrally located" and convenient but not beachfront.

WHY NOT 100:
[1] Site size and spacing issues create cramped conditions for caravans and RVs with insufficient room for awnings and outdoor living, making caravan guests feel confined despite the quality facilities. [2] Beach access requires a 10-15 minute walk that multiple families with young children found inconvenient when carrying beach gear, reducing the natural coastal experience despite the Great Ocean Road location. [3] Value perception suffers during peak periods with high nightly rates ($460 for 3-bedroom units), forced minimum stays, and additional costs for basics like extra towels, ping pong equipment, and short-duration arcade games that frustrated some families expecting better value from a premium park.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"one of the best run family parks around Australia," "immaculately clean, beautifully maintained," "cleanest Big 4 I've ever stayed in," "kids couldn't get enough of the indoor pool," "staff absolutely attentive, polite, helpful," "very cramped campsite, like rabbits in cages," "not really within walking distance to beach with kids," "we will definitely be back"

rationale_top3: BIG4 Wye River Holiday Park excels in delivering exceptional on-site entertainment for families with pedal go-karts, jumping pillow, games room, basketball court, and even a gym. Multiple families praised how kids were "entertained from dawn till dusk" with activities that kept children engaged throughout their stay. The natural environment is outstanding, with the park set alongside a creek running through the property, native wildlife including kangaroos, koalas, cockatoos and parrots visiting sites, and beach access directly across the road. The park's modern facilities, particularly the new cabins described as "comparable to a 5 star hotel room" and spotless amenities blocks, demonstrate a commitment to quality.
rationale_honourable: The location on the Great Ocean Road provides stunning natural beauty with the river meeting the ocean, perfect for exploring Lorne to Apollo Bay and nearby Otway rainforest walks. Staff receive consistent praise for being friendly and accommodating. Strong repeat visitation with families returning annually for 5-8 years demonstrates lasting appeal.
water_fun: River access and rock pools, beach across road
kids_play: Jumping pillow, pedal go-karts, games room, basketball
best_for: Families with children aged 3-12 who want abundant wildlife encounters, active outdoor play facilities, and modern cabin accommodation in a stunning natural setting along the Great Ocean Road.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Wye River Holiday Park
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 95

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Exceptional on-site activities with pedal go-karts ($5 for 30 min), jumping pillow consistently praised as highlight, games room with air hockey and car racing, basketball court, movie nights, and new gym addition. Kids described as "entertained from dawn till dusk" with families noting they "never feel the need to leave the park."

Nature (19/20) — Outstanding natural environment with creek running through park, abundant wildlife including kangaroos visiting sites, koalas in trees, cockatoos, parrots, kookaburras, and ducks. Beach directly across road, river entrance and rock pools for kids. Location where "river & country meets ocean coast" on Great Ocean Road with whale spotting opportunities.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Modern amenities described as "spotless" and "5 star clean" with heated facilities, excellent water pressure. New cabins with stone benchtops and luxury finishes. However, during peak school holidays, amenities "never seemed clean," with "floors always dirty, toilets not clean and running out of toilet paper." Some reports of inadequate servicing during busy periods.

Value (11/15) — Pricing is premium with unpowered grass sites at $92/night and cabins $250+/night drawing criticism as "highway robbery" and "very expensive even outside school holidays." Multiple charges for extras (fire pits $50, gym $10/day, go-karts, doonas). Families feel "overcharged" though many still stayed acknowledging quality.

Site Size (9/10) — Ensuite sites praised as "huge" with concrete slabs and well-grassed areas. Powered sites described as spacious with room for large rigs. However, some grass sites "literally dirt" and muddy areas noted, with site selection important for best experience.

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong repeat visitation with families returning for 5-8 years annually. Described as "best big 4 in australia" and "place of dreams we stay every year." Waitlist to get in during peak times. However, one critical flood safety incident and several negative staff interactions with younger groups noted. Most families highly recommend despite pricing concerns.

Location (10/10) — Prime Great Ocean Road position with beach across road, pub 50m away with ocean views, general store and cafe adjacent. Perfect base for exploring Lorne to Apollo Bay, Otway rainforest walks, and TreeTop Adventures within 20 minutes.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Premium pricing significantly impacts value score, with families calling it "highway robbery" at $92+ per night for unpowered sites and $250+ for cabins, plus additional charges for fire pits, gym access, and other amenities that many feel should be included.

2. Cleanliness and maintenance standards drop noticeably during peak school holiday periods, with amenities "never seemed clean," floors dirty, toilets not maintained, running out of supplies, and inadequate servicing frequency for high guest numbers.

3. Critical safety incident during 2026 floods with "lack of any warning or communication" leaving long-term guests "uninformed in a dangerous situation," and some reports of inconsistent management with younger groups receiving unwelcoming treatment including extra bonds and stereotyping.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"entertained from dawn till dusk," "comparable to a 5 star hotel room," "absolutely the best big 4 in australia," "kangaroos can come right up to cabins," "very expensive even outside school holidays," "amenities never seemed clean" (school holidays), "lack of any warning during floods," "jumping pillow was the highlight," "spotless and clearly well looked after," "highway robbery" (pricing)

rationale_top3: Outstanding natural setting with exceptional wildlife encounters - kangaroos, wallabies, and native birds throughout the grounds create magical camping experiences, with direct beach and river frontage providing authentic coastal atmosphere Excellent family entertainment facilities including giant jumping pillow, mini-golf, multiple playgrounds, pool with rock waterfall, and games room that consistently keep children engaged and entertained Premium location as the only park in Anglesea with both river and beach frontage, with 3-5 minute walk to surf beach and walking distance to town shops, cafes, and amenities, making it an ideal Great Ocean Road base
rationale_honourable: Anglesea Family Caravan Park earns Silver recognition for its outstanding natural setting with beach and river frontage, abundant wildlife including kangaroos grazing throughout the grounds, and excellent location within walking distance to town. Families consistently praise the jumping pillow, pool, multiple playgrounds, and the magical experience of waking up to wildlife, though inconsistent cleanliness during peak periods and rising prices prevent a Gold rating.
water_fun: Small pool with rock waterfall feature available
kids_play: Giant jumping pillow, mini-golf, multiple playgrounds
pet_detail: Dog friendly cabins and powered sites available; dogs restricted to specific back section of park near river; some walking restrictions apply within park grounds
best_for: This park is best suited for families who prioritize natural surroundings, wildlife encounters, and beach proximity over luxury amenities, and who don't mind rustic camping in exchange for an authentic coastal Australian experience.
executive_summary:
PARK: Anglesea Family Caravan Park
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-28
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 132

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (17/20) — Families consistently highlight the giant jumping pillow, mini-golf, multiple playgrounds, games room, pool with rock waterfall, and toddler room. Many note "heaps to keep the kids entertained" especially during peak periods, though some mention facilities close early (around 5pm) and the games room has limited appeal with only basic activities.

Nature (19/20) — Outstanding natural setting with kangaroos, wallabies, ducks, cockatoos and parrots throughout the grounds. Direct beach and river frontage with "kangaroos lying next to our tents" and wildlife encounters consistently described as magical. Bush camping sites under native trees provide authentic coastal atmosphere, with walking tracks to beach and clifftop walks nearby.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with recent reviews noting "amenities need to be cleaned more regularly at peak times" and some reports of mold, duck droppings around pool area, and toilet blocks needing refresh. Off-peak reviews praise cleanliness with "cleaned few times a day" but consistency drops during busy periods. Several 2024-2026 reviews mention facilities showing age.

Value (10/15) — Significant concern with "prices have gone thru the roof" — powered sites $93-$149/night, cabins $280-$550/night. Multiple reviews cite "overpriced" and "way overpriced in Summer Holiday period" with "value for $$ is not there now." Four-night minimums on long weekends add to cost concerns, though location partly justifies premium pricing.

Site Size (8/10) — Generally positive with "sites are big" and "lots of space" mentioned frequently. Large sites accommodate 21' vans easily, though some premium sites disappointed with "dirt and sand, very uneven" rather than grass. Bush camping sites praised for generous spacing that "don't feel crammed."

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong repeat visitation with "we come every year" appearing multiple times, and many saying "will absolutely be back." However, sentiment dampened by pricing concerns and service inconsistencies. Family reviewers overwhelmingly positive about kids' experiences, but solo travelers and those seeking premium facilities express disappointment.

Location (10/10) — Perfect score for "only park in Anglesea with both river and beach frontage." Walking distance to beach (3-5 minutes), town, shops, IGA, cafes, and bowls club. Ideal Great Ocean Road base location with 12 Apostles within 1-hour drive. "Everything is walking distance" praised universally.

WHY NOT 100:
Cleanliness inconsistency during peak periods with multiple 2024-2026 reviews reporting amenities not cleaned properly, mold in bathrooms, duck droppings around pool, and toilet blocks closing for 2-hour cleaning windows (8-10am) causing significant inconvenience. Premium pricing not matched by premium maintenance standards, with several reviews noting facilities need refresh and modernization. Value concerns are substantial with prices increasing dramatically ($93-$149 for powered sites, up to $550/night for cabins) while service quality declined, leading multiple families to state they won't return or will seek free camping alternatives despite loving the location.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kangaroos grazing near your tent," "jumping pillow, mini-golf, and multiple playgrounds," "amenities need to be cleaned more regularly at peak times," "prices have skyrocketed," "walk to the main surf beach in 3-5 minutes," "pool is very small and tiny," "heaps to keep the kids entertained," "wildlife everywhere"

rationale_top3: Nature (17/20) — Exceptional natural setting with tree-lined sites, beautiful bird life, and walking distance access to beaches, lighthouse, cliff-top walks, and coastal trails along the Great Ocean Road. Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive feedback with spotlessly clean amenities, immaculate shower blocks described as 'best shower since we left home' and 'cleanest toilet/shower block ever seen'. Entertainment (14/20) — Solid family facilities including playground, jumping pillow, heated pool, pedal carts, games room, and outdoor chess, though smaller scale than larger BIG4 parks with requests for additional features like basketball courts.
rationale_honourable: Strong location score (9/10) with walking distance to beaches, cafes, shops, and Great Ocean Road attractions. High sentiment (8/10) from many repeat visitors over multiple years. Spacious, level sites with concrete slabs available. Reasonable value for most guests despite peak season pricing concerns.
water_fun: Small heated pool, suitable for kids
kids_play: Playground, jumping pillow, pedal carts available
pet_detail: Pet friendly with dog-friendly cabins featuring enclosed yards; $10 extra per pet charge noted in some reviews.
best_for: Families with young children, nature enthusiasts, Great Ocean Road explorers, pet owners
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Aireys Inlet
SCORE: 76/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-23
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 370

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews consistently mention playground, jumping pillow, heated pool, pedal carts, games room, and outdoor chess, though several families noted the park "needs a basketball court" and some felt entertainment options were limited compared to larger BIG4 parks. The small pool size was frequently mentioned as adequate for kids but "tiny" or "dinky" by adult standards.

Nature (17/20) — Exceptional natural setting with reviews praising "waking up to birds chirping", "beautiful location surrounded by nature", "lovely bird life", and "tree-lined sites". The park offers walking distance access to beaches, lighthouse, cliff-top walks, and coastal trails, with many noting the "beautiful easy walk to the lighthouse" and "stunning beaches" nearby.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive feedback with phrases like "spotlessly clean", "immaculate amenities", "best shower since we left home", "cleanest toilet/shower block we have ever seen", and "exceptionally clean". A small number of isolated complaints about spiders, hair, or maintenance issues prevented a perfect score.

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback on pricing, with many finding it "reasonable" or "great value", but notable complaints about costs: "$730 per night is overpriced", "most expensive caravan park we stayed in", "cost has skyrocketed", and one extreme price increase complaint ($3000 to $11,000). Peak season pricing particularly noted as high.

Site Size (7/10) — Reviews describe sites as "spacious", "generous", and "level" with concrete slabs available, though some noted they were "fairly compact", "cramped", or "tight to get in". Unpowered tent sites received comments about being "small" or "dusty" with limited grass coverage.

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong positive sentiment with many repeat visitors ("4th stay", "fifth time there", "17 years", "2 years over Xmas") and high recommendation rates. However, several disappointed long-term guests mentioned policy changes, staff rudeness incidents, and service issues that detracted from otherwise positive experiences.

Location (9/10) — Excellent location praised extensively: "walking distance to everything", "cafes just across the road", "2 minute walk to general store", "short walk to beach, shops and pub", "perfect location on Great Ocean Road". Only minor criticism was road noise for cabins closest to the highway.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Limited entertainment infrastructure compared to larger BIG4 parks (no basketball court, small pool, fewer activities) with multiple reviews specifically requesting more on-site entertainment options for families.
2. Value concerns with significant pricing complaints during peak seasons and documented price increases that have deterred long-term loyal customers, with ensuite sites and cabins considered expensive relative to facilities offered.
3. Inconsistent customer service experiences including documented staff rudeness, checkout conflicts, booking disputes, and policy inflexibility that left negative impressions despite otherwise positive park qualities.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotlessly clean and fabulous stocked cabin", "best shower since we left home", "cleanest toilet/shower block by far", "walking distance to everything", "waking up to birds chirping each morning", "small peaceful holiday park with everything you need", "heated pool all year round", "friendly and helpful staff", "overpriced for what it has", "cost has skyrocketed", "staff were understanding", "never coming back"

rationale_top3: Excellent nature setting with strong nature score (20/20) on the iconic Great Ocean Road Outstanding location score (10/10) providing easy access to coastal attractions Pet-friendly facilities with maximum pet score (10/10) welcoming families with dogs
rationale_honourable: Strong cleanliness standards (11/10) indicating well-maintained facilities Good value for money (10/10) in a premium coastal location Solid entertainment options (10/10) for family activities
water_fun: Coastal location on Great Ocean Road provides access to beaches and ocean activities nearby
kids_play: Entertainment facilities available with moderate options for children
pet_detail: Highly pet-friendly park with excellent pet score, welcoming dogs and catering well to families travelling with pets
best_for: Pet owners and nature lovers seeking a Great Ocean Road coastal experience

rationale_top3: Exceptional location on the Great Ocean Road with walking distance to beach, town, shops, and the iconic Twelve Apostles, making it an ideal base for exploration Outstanding natural setting featuring a creek with suspension bridge, abundant wildlife including rabbits, and coastal walks that provide genuine nature immersion experiences Reasonable value for families who prioritize location over facilities, with some reviewers noting affordable rates despite infrastructure concerns
rationale_honourable: NRMA Port Campbell Holiday Park earns Bronze recognition primarily for its exceptional location on the Great Ocean Road, with walking distance to the beach, town, shops, and the iconic Twelve Apostles. The natural setting by the creek with a suspension bridge, abundant wildlife (rabbits), and coastal walks provides families with genuine nature immersion. While the park shows signs of aging infrastructure and inconsistent maintenance, the location and access to nature experiences keep families returning despite the operational shortcomings.
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment facilities
best_for: Families with older children who can entertain themselves exploring the beach, town, and Great Ocean Road attractions, and who don't require on-site kids' activities or pristine facilities
executive_summary:
PARK: NRMA Port Campbell Holiday Park
SCORE: 62/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-06-16
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 168

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — Reviews consistently note "no playground for the kids", "nothing for kids", "no activities for the kids", and "no swimming pool". Families report the park offers virtually no on-site entertainment, relying entirely on the town and natural environment for activities.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural setting with "walking distance to the beach", "lovely walk just outside the park with views over the town, beach, and ocean", "bridge over the creek", and "lots of cute rabbits everywhere". Multiple reviews praise "best location on the Great Ocean Rd" and proximity to Twelve Apostles. The creek, suspension bridge, and coastal walks provide genuine nature immersion.

Cleanliness (8/15) — Significant concerns with "amenities are incredibly dirty and they smell", "toilets constantly dirty", "park has fallen into disrepair", "cabin was full of spider web, the floor was a little dusty", "mould in the corners and ceiling", and "dirty bedsheets". However, some recent reviews note improvements under new management with "clean amenities" and "heated and very clean amenities".

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback with some praising "very reasonably priced" and "cost only $41 with power", while others complain "83 Dollar für eine Person und ein Auto" (overpriced for solo travelers) and "wasted $107" on unsuitable tent sites. Families note decent value when facilities work but poor value given maintenance issues.

Site Size (6/10) — Frequent complaints about "sites are tiny, ours was terribly uneven and dusty", "put us on a tiny site not suitable for a caravan", "sites smallish and quite close with no screening", and "parking was a bit tight". Premium sites receive better feedback with "big sites" and "spacious", but standard sites are clearly problematic for larger rigs.

Sentiment (7/10) — Polarized sentiment with loyal returners ("been coming here for over 30 yrs", "will be back") alongside strong negative experiences ("worst ever Holiday Park", "never coming back here again", "avoid this park if possible"). Recent reviews show improving sentiment under new management. Many appreciate location despite facility issues.

Location (10/10) — Unanimous praise for "perfect location", "in my opinion, the best location on the Great Ocean Rd", "walking distance to the beach, cafes, restaurants and shops", "short drive to the apostles", and "right next to the centre of town and the beach". The park's positioning makes it an ideal base for Great Ocean Road exploration.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of kids' entertainment facilities (no playground, no pool, no organized activities) loses 16 points in entertainment scoring, making this unsuitable for families seeking on-site activities.
2. Persistent cleanliness and maintenance issues including "dirty amenities", mould in cabins, broken fixtures, "park has fallen into disrepair", and inconsistent cleaning standards lose 7 points, with multiple reviews spanning years noting deterioration.
3. Inadequate site sizing and layout for caravans with "tiny sites", poor leveling, tight maneuvering, and lack of grass on standard sites loses 4 points, creating frustration for families with larger rigs despite premium sites being better.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best location on the Great Ocean Rd", "walking distance to the beach", "amenities are incredibly dirty", "no playground for the kids", "park has fallen into disrepair", "needs a lot of TLC", "lots of cute rabbits everywhere", "sites are tiny, ours was terribly uneven"

rationale_top3: Safety and site quality issues severely impact families—multiple reviews cite dangerous public road access through campsites with speeding cars near children's tents, jack jumper ant infestations, and cramped sites making the park unsuitable and unsafe for young families. Extremely poor value for money with consistently high prices ($70-139/night) for substandard, cramped sites with minimal facilities, no camp kitchen in many sections, and maintenance neglect that doesn't justify premium coastal pricing. Inconsistent and inadequate amenities across different park sections—'Top Bank' and 'Ocean Road' areas are essentially dirt roads with industrial backdrops, bird-infested bathrooms, no kitchen access, and poor lighting, creating a two-tier park where families risk being allocated to severely substandard areas.
rationale_honourable: Excellent natural setting beside the Erskine River with direct beach access, abundant wildlife including birds, ducks and parrots, and unbeatable central Lorne location within walking distance to all shops, restaurants and Great Ocean Road attractions.
water_fun: No pool or water facilities mentioned
kids_play: Small playground, dirty, limited kids entertainment
pet_detail: Dog friendly area available but no pets allowed over Easter; some sections not dog friendly—check specific area before booking.
best_for: Couples or adults wanting to explore Lorne town and beaches on foot, who can tolerate very basic, cramped camping conditions for the sake of central location.
executive_summary:
PARK: Lorne Foreshore Caravan Park
SCORE: 48/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 138

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — Minimal on-site entertainment for families with children. Reviews mention a "small playground" that was "dirty" with "dog poo right at the base of the slide." Multiple families noted "not much for kids" and "no room to play," with virtually no organised activities or kids' facilities. Not suitable for families seeking active children's entertainment.

Nature (15/20) — Excellent natural setting beside the Erskine River with "lots of birds and ducks," parrots, and wildlife. Direct access to the beach and river, described as "beautiful" and "peaceful." Families praised the riverside sites for nature immersion, though some sections (Top Bank, Ocean Road) lack natural appeal and are industrial/roadside settings. Close to waterfalls and coastal walks.

Cleanliness (7/15) — Highly inconsistent across the park. Many reviews cite "bird poo all over the womens showers," "amenity blocks are dirty and uncared for," "facilities were filthy," and cabins "not being properly cleaned." Some guests found amenities "exceptionally clean" and "cleaned daily," but negative cleanliness reports dominate, especially regarding bird infestations and general maintenance neglect.

Value (5/15) — Overwhelmingly poor value perception. Repeatedly described as "expensive for what you get," "most expensive park on our travels," "overpriced," and "extortionate pricing" ($70-$139/night for basic/cramped sites). Families felt the high cost didn't match the cramped, poorly maintained facilities. "Rip off" appears multiple times. Prices "nearly double on busy periods."

Site Size (3/10) — Major weakness for families. Sites consistently described as "tiny," "cramped," "laughable," "packed like sardines," with "barely enough room for tent and car." Caravans over 20ft struggled to fit. "No room to play" for children. Some sites literally on public roads with cars driving past tents. Poor for families needing space for kids or annexes.

Sentiment (5/10) — Deeply polarised sentiment. Location lovers return repeatedly, but the majority express disappointment and state they "would not stay here again" or "will not be returning." Safety concerns for children and pets, "horrific experience," "worst camping experience of my life." Negative reviews far outweigh positive ones, especially from families.

Location (9/10) — The park's standout strength. "Unbeatable" position in central Lorne with "walking distance to all the shops and restaurants," beach access within minutes, supermarket across the road, and easy access to Great Ocean Road attractions. "Can't get much better location." However, this proximity means noise from traffic and deliveries.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Safety and site quality issues severely impact families—multiple reviews cite dangerous public road access through campsites with speeding cars near children's tents, jack jumper ant infestations, and cramped sites making the park unsuitable and unsafe for young families.
2. Extremely poor value for money with consistently high prices ($70-139/night) for substandard, cramped sites with minimal facilities, no camp kitchen in many sections, and maintenance neglect that doesn't justify premium coastal pricing.
3. Inconsistent and inadequate amenities across different park sections—'Top Bank' and 'Ocean Road' areas are essentially dirt roads with industrial backdrops, bird-infested bathrooms, no kitchen access, and poor lighting, creating a two-tier park where families risk being allocated to severely substandard areas.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"worst caravan park we have ever stayed at", "cramped like sardines", "not safe for kids or pets at all", "small playground for the kids but not much else", "bird poo all over the womens showers", "expensive for what you get", "sites were terrible, mud and mulch was everywhere", "beautiful spot on Erskine River", "walking distance to all the shops and restaurants", "worst camping experience of my life", "no room to play", "great location next to the river"
